# Preface {.unnumbered}

This is a Quarto book.

To learn more about Quarto books visit <https://quarto.org/docs/books>.

Yes, you can absolutely include Jupyter Notebooks (.ipynb files) directly into a Quarto book. Quarto supports Jupyter notebooks as first-class citizens, allowing them to be rendered as chapters in books (HTML, PDF, EPUB) alongside .qmd files, preserving code, markdown, and output

control+shift+i makes a code block

What are the benefits of making them in qmd instead of juypter notebook and r markdown?

```{python}
print(1+1)
```
